key_insight: HPG-Diff combines diffusion-based topology optimization with physics-guided denoising and a differentiable connectivity penalty to produce more physically plausible structures.
engineering_relevance: The method improves generalization to unseen load/boundary conditions while reducing disconnected floating material, making it more practical for real structural design workflows.
method: Hierarchically inject precomputed physics features into the diffusion denoising process.
Use a floating-material suppression loss based on virtual heat propagation from load points.
Optionally adapt to new rectangular domains with lightweight LoRA fine-tuning.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports average compliance errors of 0.87% in-distribution and 5.29% out-of-distribution, with floating material ratios reduced to 2.90% and 2.44%.
watch_item: The connectivity constraint and physics-feature alignment add training complexity, and the reported rectangular-domain adaptation is only preliminary.
paper_title: HPG-Diff: Hierarchical physics-guided diffusion with differentiable connectivity constraints for topology optimization
